questions.q: Who killed Vali in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Rama shot the arrow that slew Vali to restore Sugriva to the throne.

questions.q: With what weapon did Rama slay Vali?
questions.explanation: Rama struck Vali with an arrow from his bow during the duel.

questions.q: Vali was the brother of which vanara who allied with Rama?
questions.explanation: Vali and Sugriva were brothers and rival kings of Kishkindha.

questions.q: Of which kingdom were Vali and Sugriva the rulers?
questions.explanation: Vali and Sugriva were the vanara kings of Kishkindha.

questions.q: Why did Rama agree to kill Vali?
options: To restore Sugriva to his throne
questions.explanation: Rama killed Vali to restore Sugriva to the throne of Kishkindha in return for Sugriva's help finding Sita.

questions.q: From where did Rama shoot the fatal arrow at Vali?
questions.explanation: Rama struck Vali with an arrow while hidden from view during the duel.

questions.q: What kind of creatures were Vali and Sugriva?
questions.explanation: Vali and Sugriva were vanaras, the monkey-folk of Kishkindha.

questions.q: What did Sugriva and Rama exchange in their alliance?
options: Help finding Sita for help regaining the throne
questions.explanation: Sugriva pledged to help Rama find Sita, and Rama pledged to kill Vali and restore Sugriva's throne.

questions.q: What was the relationship between the two combatants in the duel Rama interrupted?
questions.explanation: The duel was between the brothers Vali and Sugriva.

questions.q: Which companion of Rama was present during these events with the vanaras?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana accompanied Rama throughout his time in the forest, including the alliance with Sugriva.

questions.q: Why did Sugriva flee from Vali in the first place?
options: Vali wrongly believed Sugriva had betrayed him after a battle with a demon
questions.explanation: Vali mistakenly thought Sugriva had sealed him in a cave to die and seized his kingdom, so Sugriva fled in fear.

questions.q: What boon made Vali so formidable in single combat?
options: He could not be wounded by arrows
He drained half the strength of any foe who faced him
questions.explanation: Vali was blessed so that any opponent facing him in combat lost half his strength to Vali.

questions.q: Who was Vali's chief wife, later remarried to Sugriva?
questions.explanation: Tara, the wise queen, was Vali's wife and afterward became Sugriva's queen.

questions.q: Who was Vali's son, who later joined Rama's cause?
questions.explanation: Angada, the son of Vali and Tara, became a prince in Sugriva's service and a key vanara leader.

questions.q: Why did Rama's first arrangement to identify Vali in the duel fail?
options: Vali fled before the duel began
The brothers looked so alike that Rama could not tell them apart
questions.explanation: Vali and Sugriva resembled each other so closely that Rama could not safely distinguish them in the first encounter.

questions.q: What mark did Sugriva wear in the second duel so Rama could identify him?
questions.explanation: On Lakshmana's advice Sugriva wore a garland of gajapushpi flowers so Rama could tell him from Vali.

questions.q: Which demon's death originally led to the rift between Vali and Sugriva?
questions.explanation: Vali pursued the demon Mayavi into a cave; the misunderstanding that followed caused the brothers' bitter split.

questions.q: What was the central ethical objection Vali raised against Rama as he lay dying?
options: That Rama was not a true king
That Rama struck him unseen while he fought another, having no quarrel with Rama
That Rama had broken a vow to the gods
That Rama feared to fight openly
questions.explanation: Vali charged that Rama, a righteous prince, had wrongly killed him from hiding though Vali had done Rama no harm.

questions.q: In Rama's justification, on what grounds did he claim the right to punish Vali?
options: As an upholder of dharma punishing Vali's sin against his brother and Sugriva's wife
As a king of the vanaras
questions.explanation: Rama argued that as a prince of the Ikshvakus he could punish Vali for seizing his brother's wife and kingdom, a grave transgression.

questions.q: Whose wife had Vali wrongfully taken, deepening the charge against him?
questions.explanation: Vali had seized Ruma, Sugriva's wife, which Rama cited as part of Vali's wrongdoing.

questions.q: After Vali was slain, who was crowned king of Kishkindha?
questions.explanation: Sugriva was installed as king of Kishkindha following Vali's death.

questions.q: What role was given to Angada after Sugriva's coronation?
questions.explanation: Angada, Vali's son, was made the crown prince of Kishkindha under Sugriva.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, which Kanda (book) contains the slaying of Vali?
questions.explanation: The episode occurs in the Kishkindha Kanda, the book set in the vanara kingdom.

questions.q: By tradition, who was the divine father of Vali?
questions.explanation: Vali was regarded as a son of Indra, while Sugriva was a son of Surya.

questions.q: Who was the divine father of Sugriva?
questions.explanation: Sugriva was traditionally the son of Surya, the sun god.

questions.q: Which mighty buffalo-demon had Vali earlier defeated, demonstrating his strength?
questions.explanation: Vali had slain the buffalo-demon Dundubhi, hurling his carcass far away, a feat that figures in the legend of the duel site.

questions.q: On which sacred mountain or hill region did Sugriva take refuge from Vali, where Vali could not follow due to a curse?
questions.explanation: Sugriva hid on Rishyamukha, where Vali could not go because of a curse by the sage Matanga.

questions.q: Whose curse prevented Vali from setting foot on the hill where Sugriva sheltered?
questions.explanation: The sage Matanga had cursed Vali so that he would die if he entered the region of Rishyamukha.

questions.q: As Vali lay dying, what did Rama promise concerning his son Angada and queen Tara?
options: That they would be honored and Angada protected
That Tara would be returned to her family
questions.explanation: Rama assured the dying Vali that Angada and Tara would be cared for and Angada given an honored place.

questions.q: Which feat of strength is associated with the spot of the duel, where Rama tested his power before Sugriva trusted him?
options: Piercing seven sala trees with a single arrow
questions.explanation: To prove his might to Sugriva, Rama shot a single arrow through seven sala trees in a row.
